zh.lifeSummary: 當Amy Seeley於大約1543年出生在英國薩默塞特特斯陶頓時，她的父親William Seeley32歲，母親Elizabeth Burr27歲。她於大約1573年與William John Alford V在英國薩默塞特特斯陶頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1607年在英國英格蘭薩默塞特哈德享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Amy Seeley于大约1543年出生在英国薩默塞特特斯陶頓时，她的父亲William Seeley32岁，母亲Elizabeth Burr27岁。她于大约1573年与William John Alford V在英国薩默塞特特斯陶頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1607年在英国英格兰薩默塞特哈德享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: Seeley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for a person with a cheerful disposition, from Middle English seli ‘happy, fortunate’ (Old English sǣlig, from sǣl ‘happiness, good fortune’). The word was also occasionally used as a female personal name during the Middle Ages. The sense ‘pitiable’, which developed into modern English silly, is not attested before the 15th century. See also Selman .
Altered form of German   Seele  , respelled to preserve the bisyllabic pronunciation of the German name.

variants.name: Seely
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of   Seeley  .

variants.name: Selley
meanings: English (Devon):
variant of   Seeley  .
habitational name from Selly Oak in Northfield (Worcestershire), probably from Old English scelf ‘shelf, ledge’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

variants.name: Selman
meanings: English: nickname for a happy or fortunate man, from Middle English seli ‘happy, fortunate’ (Old English sǣlig) + man. The nickname was also used as a personal name. See also Seeley .
English: from the Middle English nickname or female personal name Sely (see Seeley ) + -man, hence an occupational name for a servant of someone so called.
Americanized form of German Sellmann (see Sellman ).

variants.name: Seelye
meanings: English: variant of   Seeley  .

variants.name: Sealy
meanings: English: variant of   Seeley  .
Welsh: from the male personal name Selyf or Seleu, medieval Welsh vernacular forms of Solomon which were replaced by that form in the Welsh Bible of 1588.
Irish: variant of   Shelley   (in counties Cork and Kerry); elsewhere of English or Welsh origin, as in 1 above and 2.

variants.name: Sealey
meanings: English: variant of   Seeley  .

variants.name: Seley
meanings: English: variant of   Seeley  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Amy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimie
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimée
meanings: originally a vernacular nickname meaning ‘beloved’, from the past participle of French aimer ‘to love’ (Latin amare; compare Amy ). It has been in use in French since the Middle Ages, although it has never been very common. It is now also used, with or without the accent, as a given name in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Amie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Amy  , corresponding in form to the French vocabulary word meaning ‘(female) friend’.

variants.name: Ami
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Amy Seeley was born about 1543, in Whitestaunton, Somerset, England, her father, William Seeley, was 32 and her mother, Elizabeth Burr, was 27. She married William John Alford V about 1573, in Whitestaunton, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1607, in Chard, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65.

ko.lifeSummary: Amy Seeley(이)가 대략 1543년 영국 서머세트 휘츠타운턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Seeley(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Burr(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 대략 1573년 영국 서머세트 휘츠타운턴에서 William John Alford V(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1607년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 처드에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Amy Seeley родилась около 1543 г. в местности уитстаунтон, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени William Seeley было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Burr, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William John Alford V около 1573 г. в местности уитстаунтон, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1607 г. в местности чард, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Amy Seeley nació aproximadamente 1543, en Whitestaunton, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, William Seeley, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Burr, tenía 27 años. Se casó con William John Alford V aproximadamente 1573, en Whitestaunton, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1607, en Chard, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 65 años.
